personally i can't see any long term ecological benefit from such overuse of pesticides but i can see long term detriment to both our land and water i could certainly continue to spell out further Name: 4_cars_car_driving_rail
Representative_Docs: ["but most major metropolitan areas in the world have much more extensive rail systems than la so it's doubtful to say it's just cost it's not just cost it's also lack of demand for rail resulting from the availability of a much better alternative mode of urban transportation cars almost all of the world's major metropolitan areas with large rail systems are old and or are located in countries where most people are still too poor to afford a car old cities were not designed for cars they were designed for walking and horse drawn vehicles and in some cases for the original electric streetcars it's very hard to retrofit these cities for the automobile age so if they're to enjoy the benefits of motorized transportation they're forced to rely on mass transit for a large share of urban travel there simply isn't enough road and parking space to allow most people to do most of their traveling by car los angeles isn't like that american cities in general aren't like that they're much newer and much more spacious a much larger fraction of them was developed after the mass affordability of cars that's why rail transit is such a poor fit for american cities in general la used to have an extensive trolley system but it was dismantled mainly in the s yes like most of the old trolley systems around the world it was dismantled because it couldn't compete with newer and better modes first motor buses and then cars i think it's virtually certain that the current la rail transit system will suffer a similar fate after self driving cars become widely available also downtown la is experiencing a major revival a huge change which many longtime la residents probably thought would never happen dtla accounts for only a small fraction of the la metro area Name: 23_greece_greek_euro_greeks
Representative_Docs: ["a greek exit would be best for all greece wasn't ready to be part of the euro spain portugal and ireland have made progress and have returned to growth italy well its trying but greece is in open rebellion greece basically want more loans with no strings attached because its economy doesn't support its social goals there's an article in ny times about the cost of greece's inept government bureaucracy and how its killing the economy worst than austerity greece should be responsible for its own economic destiny whatever that is i think the rest of europe has decided this is for the best too", 'and the e u had nothing to do with greece', "the problem with the analogy is that all those areas belong in the same political unit where the votes of the citizens in the wealthier part can also directly affect the less wealthy parts and therefore directly or indirectly manipulate their governance german voters and taxpayers have no political power to have a say in the administration of greece they are expected to hand out money but have no ability to affect how that money is spend when the germans offered to send german tax collectors and administrators to restructure the greek civil service the greeks were screaming that this would be a breach of their sovereignty because there is no political unity the eurozone had strict financial rules that members were expected to follow greece blatantly violated those rules and cooked its books so that it could keep borrowing at rates that did not reflect its true risk to accuse the greek disaster to some sinister 'banks' is disingenuous governments do not go to single banks and borrow money to be exploited by over risky bankers neither should they be compared with family units taking out a mortgage they issue bonds that are traded in markets of thousands if not hundreds of thousands of traders the interest the bond is expected to yield is derived from the evaluation of the true risk of the bond by millions of traders and no single bank is powerful enough to affect this price the problem was simple greece lied about its true financial situation borrowed far too much by pretending that it was exposed to far less risk than it really was and then expected to get even more greece at the start of the crisis had a deficit of about of gdp it was also unable to borrow any money at all from the private markets because its risk was now recognised for the level it was that is what started the crisis the recognition that greece could not borrow any more it was not and it is not the level of debt that is the problem the problem is that greece effectively has the economic productivity of a third world african country that was propped up by excessive borrowing pumped into the economy for consumption rather than productive investment the problem is that greece relies on excessive deficit spending to retain living standards of a first world country which it is not had greece been left on its own and had to immediately stop injecting its economy with an amount equivalent to of its gdp the chain reaction would have probably led to its economy shrinking by half within weeks with certainly far worse effects than what has occurred gradually in the last years what the troika did was take on the private debt that greece had so that it could be serviced at much lower interest than what it would otherwise have to be and also allowed greece to borrow at reduced interest rates so that it would not have to cut its spending abruptly and lead to the chain reaction that would have otherwise devastated it greece pays interest of around of its debt far lower than its true risk and in fact lower than many healthier countries in the eurozone this together with a massive debt write off and forgiveness in this together with the billions in free money that greece has been taking and squandering from eu structural funds for years and yet greeks complain that the bailout 'helped the banks and not greece' let's not forget that all the money that went to the banks is money that greece had already borrowed and had already spend and thus was already obligated to pay back if i've been leaving off of borrowed money for decades and my family comes and pays my debts for me allows me to pay the family back at much more lenient terms than before and let's me keep borrowing a tiny bit at a reduced rate until i can get a job for myself i recognize that is help what the troika asked in return is that greece start to control its spending which is something that would have happened anyway had the troika not helped but which without the troika would have happened immediately and with far more shocking consequences and that it reforms its economy to become more competitive successive greek governments did little to nothing to truly reform the economy and the current government has been steadily reversing back any good reform that might have been made in the last few years greek remains uncompetitive corrupt difficult to do business in and at the same time let's not pretend that had the banks not been paid that this would not have consequences if a bank cannot have loans it has made repaid this affects the people who have deposited in the bank or invested in its instruments that's depositors that's pension funds that's private companies and investment funds who manage the money of private companies who if they lose their money will have to fire off people why should all of these people lose their money just because greece cooked its books so that it could borrow more than it could spend neither is it the fault of the euro or the eu or anything of the sort the euro was not some stealthy instrument of destruction that snuck up on greece every successive greek government for years knew exactly what the value of the euro was and given the economic environment they were in what would be good and bad policy in dealing with the euro every government consecutively followed the route of bad policy the euro is priced far too low for the german economy which helps it being an export oriented country the euro if priced far too high for the greek economy which would have helped given that it is an import oriented economy so long as it was spend on imports of goods and services to be spend productively by being in a strong union greece was also able to borrow at more lenient terms than it otherwise would have had it still had the drachma again a good thing had it borrowed to spend on productive projects and investments greece instead borrowed money to spend on the wages of public servants doing nothing and imported goods for pure consumption and had any greek government campaigned on a reversal of this course they would not have been voted by the electorate germany cannot be faulted for examining the structure of the euro and making good policy decisions based on it and greece cannot be excused for doing the same things and making bad decisions ps i'm greek don't try to judge the country from outside with political biases from different countries greece is what people say it is full of corruption inefficiency a massive and useless public sector clientelism and everything else personally i'd think that a grexit would be help the economy improve if i could trust that it would be managed by a government also ready to take the drastic reforms needed to make the country competitive but what will happen is default and management by a government with all the wrong ideas and all the policies to drive greece to the dark age if there's one thing that's good about the bailout is that at least there's somebody from above to try to keep pushing for institutional restructuring even if most greeks scream and curse at them"]

Name: 34_neutrality_comcast_internet_net
Representative_Docs: ["i want to make it clear what the fcc's murdering of net neutrality means it means that comcast executives get to pick and choose to suit their personal biases with net neutrality dead comcast can make comcast's websites load fast and make mother jones load slow or they could make republican websites load fast and make democratic websites load slow even if mother jones and the democrats offer to pay for fast service and the republicans don't pay anything without net neutrality comcast can actually do that and what are you going to do if they do it there's no competition comcast is the only broadband provider in most places where it is", 'given that the comment is reasonably well written i m betting this person is actually a paid industry shill the person says for years netflx has been paying rd party internet delivery services such as cogent and level to deliver streaming video to netflix subscribers no absolutely not netflix does use cogent and level for their bandwidth exactly like you d use an isp at home but on a much much much larger scale nothing is delivered directly to netlfix subscribers that is just not how the internet works what is an internet delivery service anyway that phrase there is an attempt to mislead that is not a term of art in my field infrastructure engineering nor in any related field i ve used both cogent and level bought loads of bandwidth from them in fact i bought so much bandwidth from cogent in nj they had to run an entirely new fiber line cogent and level provide netflix s bandwidth just as your isp provides yours that s all that s happening these companies deliver the streaming video to isps such as comcast verizon att et al through commercial agreements based on the amount of video transmitted back and forth between the isps and the rd party delivery services i e you take my data transmissions i ll take yours and we will call it even as netflix became more popular the flow of video became more one sided from netflix and the rd party services to the isps the isps want compensation for the imbalanced data exchange your careful engineering point i can tell this guy is a shill as there is enough truth mixed with the falsehoods that he is obviously technically knowledgeable to some extent that s why i suspect the person is actually in industry employ let s be clear here though what shill is talking about is peering agreements which are indeed made between backbone providers however comcast is not and never has been a backbone provider it is a last mile provider peering agreements are not made between last mile providers and backbone providers because bandwidth here is almost always asymmetrical and why is this it s because almost anyone on any isp except for a very few have much more downstream than upstream bandwidth allocation for instance comcast s mbs service only offers mbs upstream which is less than as fast traffic from a residential isp can t help but be asymmetrical since not only is upstream bandwidth much lower than downstream in fact most of the time to force us to be good little consumers you can t even run servers from your home per the tos this traffic then will always always be asymmetrical and peering agreements which presume roughly symmetrical bandwidth would never work what really happened is that netflix is in competition with comcast and more and more of comcast s customers started requesting traffic of content hosted by netflix comcast deliberately degraded this content by i m guessing here i have no details of internal comcast network management reducing the number of agg ports the level or cogent bandwidth flows through at some crucial routing center that s the way i d do it anyway if i were an evil isp that way they can argue that they aren t actually prioritizing traffic the hole just isn t big enough for the bandwidth which is correct as they ve made it too small all without formal traffic management of any sort traditionally what a residential isp would do if their customers started requesting more traffic than could be delivered would be to upgrade their infrastructure say comcast customers are requesting tbs at peak times and most of this flows through cogent as its backbone provider comcast would say to cogent this link is getting saturated can we drop another fiber from our core router to yours in poughkeepsie often these crucial internet routing points are in the same datacenters by the way just for this ease of functioning and upgrading as needed and cogent would say ok it ll be ready in a week please have the datacenter plug it into port on patch panel g then a new fiber would go across the datacenter from cogent s racks to comcast s racks and tbs available traffic would become tbs just like that in the cast of netflix though this wasn t even necessary comcast was offered and refused netfix s caching boxes that would ve made the traffic free to them essentially thus comcast chose to punish their own customers which seems a bit of modus operandi for them in other words this whole problem could ve been solved if comcast had accepted for free a few very expensive servers it would not even have required the traditional infrastructure upgrades in other words as the demand has increased the quality of the rd party delivery has declined netflix has allowed their customers to get crappy service from the rd party delivery services in order to put pressure on the isps to do a delivery deal directly with netflix this is not how the internet works netflix has absolutely no desire to do a deal with this isps directly none that is harmful to them and always will be these rd party delivery services shill is talking about are level cogent et al and they always provide exactly as much bandwidth you ask for it is their job and they honestly do it pretty well they are very good at it if you order gbs you get it etc no it is comcast that did not upgrade its
